events.title: Fairfax Book Club: On the Wings of a Cuckoo by Kokila Byrne
events.description: The Fairfax Library Thursday Morning Book Club will meet in-person every other month to discuss a new book. Everyone is welcome to attend whether or not you have read the selected book for that meeting. This is a drop-in event. We gather in person at the Fairfax Library for this conversation.We will be discussing our selection: On the Wings of a Cuckoo by Kokila Byrne.On the Wings of a Cuckoo is written by Fairfax's own Kokila Byrne. Join our discussion and gain insight from the author herself!"In 1974, Kokila (Mary Ann Byrne) embarked on a life-changing journey, leaving her teaching career in the United States to travel to India with her five-year-old son. Over the next decade, they immersed themselves in a rich tapestry of diverse cultures, from the beaches of Goa in southern India to the majestic Himalayan mountains. After three transformative years, they joined the ashram of Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh, where Kokila welcomed the birth of her daughter. During their time in the East, Kokila and her family explored many countries. This extraordinary adventure shaped her worldview and deepened her connection to the human spirit. Returning to the United States in 1984, Kokila resumed her teaching career, yet her wanderlust and quest for understanding remained undiminished. Each summer, she embarked on new journeys to distant lands, continually weaving her life with rich experiences and a global perspective. Kokila's writing reflects profound insights gained from years of exploration, spiritual growth, and cross-cultural encounters. Her work invites readers to join her on a journey of discovery-both outward into the world and inward to the depths of the soul."--AmazonTo acquire a copy of this book, please call Fairfax Library at 415-453-8151.Fairfax Book Club is sponsored by the Friends of the Fairfax Library. events.title: April Moraga Book Club

Tilar J. Mazzeo brings to life the woman behind the label, Barbe-Nicole Clicquot Ponsardin, in this utterly intoxicating book that is as much a fascinating journey through the process of making this temperamental wine as a biography of a uniquely tempered and fascinating woman."Note that Moraga Book Club is for adults ages 18 and up. Participants are encouraged to read or listen to the first book in the girl’s story (this month The Big Break) to be able to join in on the discussion. Each month we will play games, do a craft, and may have a light snack inspired by each girl’s life. In order to help everyone feel included, we kindly ask that you leave your personal dolls at home! events.title: Book Banter: Why We Sleep
events.description: Join us for a fun, engaging discussion of Why We Sleep, by Matthew Walker, PhD. --- Neuroscientist and sleep expert Matthew Walker provides a revolutionary exploration of sleep, examining how it affects every aspect of our physical and mental well-being. Charting the most cutting-edge scientific breakthroughs, and marshalling his decades of research and clinical practice, Walker explains how we can harness sleep to improve learning, mood and energy levels, regulate hormones, prevent cancer, Alzheimer's and diabetes, slow the effects of aging, and increase longevity. He also provides actionable steps towards getting a better night's sleep every night.

events.title: Mary Ann Key Book Club Discussion: The Serviceberry
events.description: The Mary Ann Key Book Club uses reading as a catalyst for intercultural conversations around understanding and addressing past and current injustices that impact Black, Indigenous, and People of Color. Join your neighbors in a discussion of our 2026 season selection: The Serviceberry by Robin Wall Kimmerer. While Indigenous scientist and author Robin Wall Kimmerer harvests serviceberries alongside the birds, she considers the ethic of reciprocity that lies at the heart of the gift economy and how we can learn from Indigenous wisdom and the plant world to reimagine what we value most. Copies of the book can be reserved; eBooks and eAudiobooks are available through www.hclib.org. Sponsor: Friends of the Hennepin County Library.

Everyone in My Family Has Killed Someone by Benjamin Stevenson SYNOPSIS: Some of us, the high achievers, have killed more than once. I'm not trying to be dramatic, but it is the truth. Some of us are good, others are bad, and some just unfortunate. I'm Ernest Cunningham. Call me Ern or Ernie. I wish I'd killed whoever decided our family reunion should be at a ski resort, but it's a little more complicated than that. Have I killed someone? Yes. I have. Who was it? Let's get started.

Miller who will be sharing her current project.March 12: Feeding Ghosts by Tessa Hulls (2024), 400 pagesApril 9: Yellow Cab by Benoit Cohen (2022), 168 pagesMay 14: 40 Men and 12 Rifles: Indochina 1954 by Marcelino Truong (2023), 296 pagesTitles can be borrowed from the Palo Alto City Library and through our Link+ system - click on the links above or search in our catalog for more information. Meetings are held in person in the Midtown Room at Mitchell Park Library and facilitated by Danièle Archambault. The GNBC has been meeting since 2015! Danièle Archambault, Ph.D. (Linguistics), is a researcher, author, and educator who is interested in comics as a way to document a society's cultural and linguistic landscape. Originally from Montreal, Quebec, she now lives in Palo Alto, where she is an artist in residence at the Cubberley Artist Studio Program, a program of the City of Palo Alto. In the past few years, she has published several full-color comic books, graphic novels, and electronic books. Ms. Archambault is a regular guest speaker on documenting culture and dialects through comics at various educational and cultural institutions. She teaches Visual Storytelling at the Palo Alto Art Center, and at other educational venues. http://danielebd.com/

events.title: Xenia Community Book Club
events.description: Join us to discuss The Frozen River by Ariel Lawhon. Everyone is welcome! --- Copies of the book are available at the information desk at your Xenia Community Library. If you prefer an eBook or eAudiobook, check Libby and Hoopla.About the book:"A gripping historical mystery based on the real-life diary entries of Martha Ballard, an 18th-century midwife who defied the legal system and wrote herself into American history. Maine, 1789: When the Kennebec River freezes, entombing a man in the ice, Martha Ballard is summoned to examine the body and determine cause of death. As a midwife and healer, she is privy to much of what goes on behind closed doors in Hallowell. Her diary is a record of every birth and death, crime and debacle that unfolds in the close-knit community. Months earlier, Martha documented the details of an alleged rape committed by two of the town's most respected gentlemen— one of whom has now been found dead in the ice. But when a local physician undermines her conclusion, declaring the death to be an accident, Martha is forced to investigate the shocking murder on her own. Over the course of one winter, as the trial nears, and whispers and prejudices mount, Martha doggedly pursues the truth. Her diary soon lands at the center of the scandal, implicating those she loves, and compelling Martha to decide where her own loyalties lie."

events.description: Join us for a meaningful, engaging discussion hosted in English, Farsi, and French. In March, we'll discuss Alice's Adventures in Wonderland by Lewis Carroll, and in April we'll dive into Where the Wild Things Are by Maurice Sendak. Participants will reflect on the messages of each book and share their insights in a supportive environment. Presented in partnership with Lantern Book Club. Teens welcome. برای یک گفت‌وگوی جذاب و معنا‌دار به زبان‌های انگلیسی، فارسی و فرانسوی، به ما بپیوندید.در ماه مارس، کتاب «آلیس در سرزمین عجایب» و در ماه آوریل، «جایی که موجودات وحشی هستند» را بررسی خواهیم کرد.شرکت‌کنندگان درباره پیام هر کتاب فکر می‌کنند و دیدگاه‌های خود را در فضایی دوستانه و حمایتی به اشتراک می‌گذارند.این برنامه با همکاری گروه Lantern Book Club برگزار می‌شود.نوجوانان عزیز، خوش‌آمدید!Pour une discussion enrichissante et captivante en anglais, farsi et français, rejoignez-nous. One unavoidable, unpredictable destiny. This is the beginning of the end. In the SeaWing kingdom, a young prince learns he is an animus, capable of wonderful magic that comes with a terrible price. In the mind of a NightWing dragonet, a thousand futures unfold and almost all of them, she knows, lead to disaster and destruction. And under three full moons and the watchful eyes of his NightWing mother and IceWing father, the most powerful dragon Pyrhhia will ever know is clawing his way out of his egg: Darkstalker, the dragon who will change the world forever. Long before the SandWing war, lifetimes before the Dragonet Prophecy, darkness is born."

events.title: Graphic Novel Club: On Guard!
events.description: Graphic novel pick for this month is: On Guard! by Cassidy Wasserman We will have limited copies available for check out at the Ask Me Desk. Description from library catalog:"On guard! Ready? FENCE! Grace isn't looking forward to seventh grade. Her best friend, Ava, dropped her at the end of last year, the dust is still settling from her parents' messy divorce, and things are not going well between Grace and her mom. Mothers and daughters are so close in movies, but that's never been Grace and her mom. So now school and home both feel complicated. In fact, there's not a single place Grace can just relax. Until she sees the school's Fencing Club practicing. They look so sure of themselves. So steady. Not to mention their supercool...swords? Needles? What are they called?! Grace is pretty sure she wants to find out! It may not solve everything...but could this be a place Grace can finally belong and be herself?"On Guard!

And if our current theme isn't for you, don't fret - check back in July for our next topic!This unit's focus is "Fantastical History", and April's selection is "Tread of Angels" by Rebecca Roanhorse (201 pages). events.title: Teen Writing Workshop
events.description: Have you always dreamed of one day writing a book? Maybe you have a few essays or stories written up and want feedback from other writers. Or perhaps you want to improve your skills by doing fun creative writing exercises! If that sounds like a good time to you, drop by this writing club! Everyone is welcome - from beginners to experienced authors. This group is for teens only. In this workshop we will: -Read targeted pieces to generate ideas about theme, plot twists, and writing techniques. -Peer review each other's work. -Do fun writing exercises. -Encourage each other to conquer writer's block and gain confidence in sharing our writing. This workshop will take place on two Saturdays per month. Registration recommended but not required. Library staff may take photos, videos, or audio recordings at our events. Please tell us if you do not wish to be photographed, video-recorded, or audio-recorded. We will always respect your requests An ASL (American Sign Language) interpreter or closed captioning can be provided for this program if requested at least seven business days in advance. For assistance, please contact your library or accessibility@aclibrary.org.

events.title: Between the Sheets: A Romance Book Club
events.description: If you love talking tropes, subgenres, HEA versus HFN then this is the book club for you. Every month we'll read a different romance book and come together to discuss what we loved or hated. Books will range from closed door to explicit and explore a wide variety of subgenres. All are welcome! This month's book is Ayesha at Last by Uzma Jalaluddin.Ayesha Shamsi has a lot going on. Her dreams of being a poet have been set aside for a teaching job so she can pay off her debts to her wealthy uncle. She lives with her boisterous Muslim family and is always being reminded that her flighty younger cousin, Hafsa, is close to rejecting her one hundredth marriage proposal. Though Ayesha is lonely, she doesn't want an arranged marriage. Then she meets Khalid who is just as smart and handsome as he is conservative and judgmental. She is irritatingly attracted to someone who looks down on her choices and dresses like he belongs in the seventh century. When his engagement to Hafsa is announced, Ayesha must deal with the truth about Khalid, her family... and herself. Steam Level: 🔥/5 Ayesha at LastDML welcomes everyone to our programs. If you have questions or need any accommodations, please contact us so we can help make the program a good experience for you.Check out our social story to familiarize yourself with the Miami Township Branch!

events.title: Mary Ann Key Book Club Discussion: The Serviceberry
events.description: The Mary Ann Key Book Club uses reading as a catalyst for intercultural conversations around understanding and addressing past and current injustices that impact Black, Indigenous, and People of Color. Join your neighbors in a discussion of our 2026 season selection: The Serviceberry by Robin Wall Kimmerer. While Indigenous scientist and author Robin Wall Kimmerer harvests serviceberries alongside the birds, she considers the ethic of reciprocity that lies at the heart of the gift economy and how we can learn from Indigenous wisdom and the plant world to reimagine what we value most. Copies of the book can be reserved; eBooks and eAudiobooks are available through www.hclib.org. Sponsor: Friends of the Hennepin County Library.

events.title: Food For Thought: A Culinary Book Club
events.description: Indulge in your passion for literature and cuisine at a unique book club where we combine the love of reading with the love of food! For April, we're going to read different books based on a specific theme chosen for the month. Come ready to talk about the book you have read and get some new ideas for your own reading. We are still going to be making and eating a delicious snack inspired by the monthly theme. Don't miss out on this opportunity to bond with fellow book-lovers over a shared love of literature and food.The April theme is Regeneration, Rebirth, and Hope. Come ready to talk about a book that has inspired you to grow, change, or given you hope.To accompany our book talks we will be cooking and eating omelettes.Here's some book suggestions to get you started. All these titles are available at the Library. Life After Life by Kate Atkinson Glory: A Novel by NoViolet Bulawayo Life of Pi by Yann Martel How to Build a Boat by Elaine Feeney James by Percival Everett Pearl by Sian Hughes Wilding by Isabella Tree The Marrow Thieves by Cherie Dimaline Parable of the Sower by Octavia E. Butler The Midnight Library by Matt Haig NOTE: Registration is required and there is a $5 charge for this class. Payment can be made via Zeffy. A link will be sent to the email address you provide when you complete the registration process on this page.

events.title: Non-Fiction Book Discussion: The Showman:inside the invasion that shook the world and made a leader of Volodymyr Zelensky
events.description: Book Cover of The ShowmanOpen to all adults who love to read and discuss Non-fictional literature. In April the group will talk about The showman: inside the invasion that shook the world and made a leader of Volodymyr Zelensky by Simon Schuster (January 2024) Time Magazine correspondent Simon Shuster drew on the unprecedented degree of access he was allowed to President Zelensky and his government and military to write The Showman, including interviews with Zelensky ’s allies, his wife Olena, and even his opponents to tell the story of how a naiive comedian, actor and satirist made the unlikely transition to steadfast Ukrainian military leader. A man and a head of state who has somehow won allies in the West and used their financial support to protect his country and his people from Vladimir Putin and the invading Russian Army. Shuster does not try to make Zelensky a saint or a perfect hero. He and those he interviewed talk about Zelensky’s early stumbles in office, his initial naivete and lack of political experience; his sensitivity to criticism. During the war, Shuster mention’s Zelenskyy’s crackdowns on the Ukrainian Press, and his sometimes contentious relations with even his allies on account of his constant need of more financial aid. Even with all of his shortcomings Zelensky remarkably rose to the challenge of becoming the leader Ukraine needed when he was needed most.The Nonfiction Book Discussion Group meets on the second Sunday morning of each month at 11am. All meetings are sponsored by The Friends of the Cupertino Library.

Be ready to read your favorite passages to the group! If the books on this list look too difficult. Try our Intermediate ESL Book Club. Here's the reading list: January: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time February: Like Water for Chocolate March: A Council of Dolls April: The Art Thief May: The Mystery of Mrs. Christie June: Dreams From My Father July: The Secret Book of Frida Kahlo August: The Last Thing He Told Me September: The Brightest Star October: Mrs. Lincoln's Dressmaker November: The Personal Librarian December: The Henna Artist Any person needing a reasonable accommodation for a disability to access the benefits of the Library’s services, programs, or activities under the Americans with Disabilities Act should contact the Library by telephone at (972) 292-5669, by email at library@friscotexas.gov, or in writing, not less than five (5) business days prior to the program.

events.title: High Intermediate ESL Book Club - The Murderbot Diaries Vol 1
events.description: Our high intermediate ESL Book Club will read The Murderbot Diaries Vol 1 by Martha Wells. This book club is best for high intermediate English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then we will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Sheila Jurik. Meeting datesJanuary 12, 26 (No class Jan 19)February 2, 23 (No class Feb 9, 16)March 2, 9, 23, 30 (No class Mar 16)April 13, 20, 27 (No class Apr 6)Meeting time: 10:15am - 11:45amWhere: In the library - your book club leader will tell you which tables. Registration is open now! Book club is limited to 10 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting. The Murderbot Diaries Vol 1 description:On a distant planet, a team of scientists are conducting surface tests, shadowed by their Company-supplied ‘droid ― a self-aware SecUnit that has hacked its own governor module, and refers to itself (though never out loud) as “Murderbot.” Scornful of humans, all it really wants is to be left alone long enough to figure out who it is (and to watch its favorite show in its downtime.) But when a neighboring mission goes dark, it’s up to the scientists and Murderbot to get to the truth.

events.title: Manga Monday Book Club
events.description: Enjoy reading manga? Join other teens to discuss stories and characters in our new Manga Monday Club! We will introduce Volume 1 of a series to get you started on your next reading adventure. We encourage teens to read at least Volume 1, whether that is a physical copy from your own manga collection, from our library, or borrowed from a friend. Digital reading also counts; we will be happy to have you participate in our discussion if you have read an e-copy. Physical copies of Volume 1 will be available for in person pick-up at the Enon Branch on a first come, first served basis. Check out Libby and Hoopla for available digital copies. Scheduled for the second Monday of the month. For teens. This month's selection: Witch Hat Atelier by Kamome Shirahama Summary: In a world where everyone takes wonders like magic spells and dragons for granted, Coco is a girl with a simple dream: She wants to be a witch. But everybody knows magicians are born, not made, and Coco was not born with a gift for magic. Resigned to her un-magical life, Coco is about to give up on her dream to become a witch...until the day she meets Qifrey, a mysterious, traveling magician. After secretly seeing Qifrey perform magic in a way she's never seen before, Coco soon learns what everybody "knows" might not be the truth, and discovers that her magical dream may not be as far away as it may seem...” Rated 13 +

events.title: Teen Advisory Committee (TAC)
events.description: Join the Teen Advisory Committee (TAC): Lead, Serve, and Create! Want to make your voice heard and help shape what happens at your library? The Stratford Branch Library is looking for motivated teens to join the Teen Advisory Committee (TAC)—a team of young leaders who help connect local youth with the library and the wider community. As a TAC member, you won’t just attend meetings—you’ll help plan programs, influence decisions, and create a better space for teens through service, advocacy, and leadership. Why Join TAC? Joining TAC isn’t just another club—it’s a leadership opportunity that can help you grow personally and professionally. ✨ Earn Volunteer Hours Receive official service hours for attending monthly meetings and supporting library programs. 🚀 Build Real Skills Gain hands-on experience in event planning, teamwork, public speaking, and project management—great additions to college and scholarship applications. 📚 Exclusive Perks Get first access to new Young Adult (YA) books and Manga arrivals Receive a Letter of Recommendation after completing your term 🎯 Make Real Decisions Help guide library choices—from suggesting new YA and Manga titles to managing a small committee for snacks, décor, or children's events. What You’ll Do As a TAC member, you’ll act as a teen consultant for the library, helping staff understand what teens want and need. 🎮 Plan Events Help brainstorm and host activities like gaming tournaments, craft nights, or even a mini “Comic-Con” program. 📣 Advocate for Teens Recommend new books and Manga and help improve teen spaces in the library. 📱 Promote & Connect Create social media content, spread the word about events, and collaborate with local school clubs.Interested in Joining?Fill out a form on hcpl.net https://hcpl.net/get-involved/ or come to a meeting.

Please join us in the Upstairs Conference Room on the second Monday of the month to read silently as a group without any discussion and enjoy light refreshments provided by the Friends of Freeman Library. The door opens at 6:00 PM. No registration is necessary for this free program.

events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description: A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.小朋友们，你们听说过《西游记》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的孙悟空、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的猪八戒，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关 美猴王系列丛书. 免费的亲子中文活動. 座位数量有限. 门票将在故事时间开始前 30 分钟在询问处领取。 圖書館中文资源: 大人最新中文书, 中文電子書, 非小說類電子書 (Overdrive) 有歡迎大家一起來借閱電子書. Overdirve平台: 小朋友的中文電子書, 大朋友的中文電子小說, 大朋友的非小說類的電子書籍. Hoopla平台: 中文有聲書籍 美国残疾人法案(ADA)申请提供协助 5/25/2026

events.title: SciFi & Fantasy Book Club
events.description: Do you love delving into mysterious and fantastical worlds? Are intricate magic systems and complex alien civilizations your thing? Or are you in it for characters and themes not so different from our own lives, sorcery or space life notwithstanding? If so, then join our Science Fiction and Fantasy Book Club at San Mateo County Libraries! We'll meet every second Monday of the month at 6:30 PM at the Foster City Library. Want to know what we're reading? Click the registration link below to view an up-to-date reading list as well as our past titles. REGISTER HERE

events.title: Science Fiction and Fantasy Book Club - Untethered Sky by Fonda Lee
events.description: Explore strange new worlds and alternate realities by joining the Science Fiction and Fantasy Book Club at Milpitas Library! Sign up and pick up a copy of Untethered Sky by Fonda Lee . Read it and meet us on Monday, April 13th at 7pm in the Auditorium of Milpitas Library. "In a world of monsters, some bonds are forged in blood and sky." Ester’s life changes forever when a manticore destroys her family, leaving her with a burning desire for vengeance. She finds her purpose as a ruhker, a trainer of giant, majestic rocs, bred to hunt the deadly manticores that plague the land. As Ester forms an unbreakable bond with her roc, Zara, she learns that survival demands sacrifice, and victory comes at a cost. A stunning tale of courage, loss, and the unyielding bond between human and beast, Untethered Sky soars with heart-pounding action and unforgettable emotion. For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org

events.title: Reading Bingo
events.description: Join us in celebrating Día del Niño (Day of the Child) at the Union City Library throughout April. Pick up a "Reading Bingo" card at the reference desk and complete five reading activities across or diagonally on your own or with a reading friend. Show us your completed bingo card at the reference desk by Saturday, May 2, 2026, for a small prize, while supplies last. Día del Niño (Day of the Child) is celebrated in Mexico every year on April 30. It is a holiday that highlights the wonder and joy children bring into our lives. The library has adapted Día del Niño; we celebrate Día del Niño & Libro by promoting reading and children’s literacy. Acompáñanos a celebrar el Día del Niño en la Biblioteca de Union City durante todo el mes de abril. Recoge tu tarjeta de "Reading Bingo" en el mostrador de consulta y completa cinco actividades de lectura tú solo o con un compañero. Enséñanos tu tarjeta terminada y recibirás un premio pequeño mientras duran existencias. El Día del Niño es celebrado en México cada 30 de abril. Es un día festivo que resalta la maravilla y alegría que los niños comparten con nosotros. El Día del Niño ha sido adaptado por la biblioteca, celebraremos el Día del Niño & Libro promoviendo la lectura y alfabetización infantil. Library staff may take photos, videos, or audio recordings at our events. Please tell us if you do not wish to be photographed, video-recorded, or audio-recorded. We will always respect your requests! An ASL (American Sign Language) interpreter or closed captioning can be provided for this program if requested at least seven (7) days in advance. For assistance, please contact your library or accessibility@aclibrary.org.

events.title: Seniors Social
events.description: Join us for a relaxed and welcoming Seniors Social designed to support activity, connection, and community. This free program offers a mix of activity, games, and social time in a friendly, inclusive space. events.title: Bookmarked Book Club
events.description: This month’s selection is The Shadow of the Wind by Carlos Ruiz Zafón. It follows Daniel Sempere, who discovers a mysterious book by author Julián Carax and embarks on a quest to find other works, uncovering a dark secret involving murder, madness, and doomed love as he realizes someone is systematically destroying Carax's books. The novel is a literary thriller praised for its atmospheric setting, complex plot, and themes of love, loss, and the power of literature.The Shadow of the Wind

events.title: Virtual Author Talk: Brad Taylor
events.description: Shadow Strike erupts from page one as a rogue faction within the Iranian regime unleashes a bold act of retaliation. Brad Taylor delivers a high‑stakes thriller in which a rogue faction inside the Iranian regime launches a bold plan for retaliation, unleashing a legendary assassin whose mission threatens to ignite a global crisis. When the Ghost escapes during a prison transfer, Pike Logan and his team are pulled into a relentless pursuit that spans from the Middle East to Argentina, working alongside Mossad to uncover the true scope of an operation designed to destabilize international power structures. As the Taskforce races through Iguazu Falls, the Triple Frontier, Buenos Aires, and down to Ushuaia at the “End of the World,” it becomes clear that the threat reaches far beyond a single assassination attempt. The Ghost’s mission carries consequences that could strike at the heart of the United States, forcing Pike to confront an enemy whose success would reshape the global balance of power. ---The Santa Clara County Library District is proud to present a free virtual author‑talk series featuring bestselling, award‑winning, and acclaimed writers from around the world, offering live Q&A sessions two to three times a month. Event links are sent about 24 hours in advance (or upon late registration), recordings are available for a limited time, and a full list of upcoming programs can be found here. ---For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.title: Meet Mike Curato, Author of Gaysians
events.description: San Mateo County Libraries is excited to welcome acclaimed author and artist Mike Curato, as he discusses his heart-warming adult debut adult graphic novel Gaysians. On Tuesday, April 14 from 5:30 PM to 6:00 PM, join us for a virtual Q&A session with Mike – grab a library copy of Gaysians ahead of time and jot down all your insightful questions! Limited copies of Gaysians will be raffled away to Zoom attendees after the event. Enjoy reading gripping graphic novels and want to find a few more reads? Check out our Peninsula Libraries Comic Arts Fest, where we celebrate local comics creators all throughout the month of April! About the Book From the acclaimed author of the YA graphic novel Flamer, comes a gorgeous tale of four gay Asians as they navigate love, identity, and friendship in Seattle during the early aughts. When AJ moves to Seattle, he’s ready to reinvent himself as a gay Asian man—but his dreams hit reality fast with no friends, no job, and an apartment so far out, “not even lesbians live there.” Then a spilled drink at a bar introduces him to K, a glamorous drag queen; John, a shy gamer; and Steven, a reckless flirt. AJ’s “Boy Luck Club” helps him find love, pride, and belonging—until a brutal attack tests everything they know about friendship and family. Meticulously observed and gorgeously illustrated, Gaysians is a fierce, funny, and tender story of queer resilience and self-discovery. Read Mike’s blog post to learn more about why he wrote this book. Meet Mike Curato Mike Curato is an author and illustrator of books for children, teens, and adults. He is best known for his Little Elliot picture book series and his young adult graphic novel, Flamer, which received several honors including the 2020 Lambda Literary Award for LGBTQ Young Adult, the 2021 Massachusetts Book Award for Young Adult, and was listed as one of NPR’s Best Books of 2020. Flamer also has the distinction of being one of the most banned and challenged books in the United States, and Mike has become a fierce advocate of free speech. He continues to create books that center love, compassion, and friendship. Mike’s debut adult graphic novel, Gaysians, released in June of 2025 to critical acclaim. REGISTER HERE

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: How to Pronounce Knife
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Join our book club for spirited discussions on a wide range of fiction and non-fiction. Each month we'll tackle a new title. Whether you loved it, loathed it, or left it unfinished, we want to hear your take. Come share your impressions and connect with fellow readers! This week we will be reading How to Pronounce Knife by Souvankham ThammavongsaA young man painting nails at the local salon. A woman plucking feathers at a chicken processing plant. A father who packs furniture to move into homes he'll never afford. A housewife learning English from daytime soap operas. In her stunning Giller Prize-winning debut book of fiction, Souvankham Thammavongsa focuses on characters struggling to make a living, illuminating their hopes, disappointments, love affairs, acts of defiance, and above all their pursuit of a place to belong.

events.title: The Page Turners
events.description: The Page Turners is an evening book discussion group for adults that meets at the Castro Valley Library on the second Tuesday of the month at 7 pm. Facilitated by librarian Chris Selig, the discussions focus on compelling, well-reviewed fiction and nonfiction books written for adults.For April we will discuss the Fagin the Thief by Allison Epstein, which is available for check out through the Library collection and Link Plus.SynopsisLong before Oliver Twist stumbled onto the scene, Jacob Fagin was scratching out a life for himself in the dark alleys of nineteenth-century London. Born in the Jewish enclave of Stepney shortly after his father was executed as a thief, Jacob and his open-minded mother, Leah, are each other's whole world. But Jacob's prospects are forever altered when a light-fingered pickpocket takes Jacob under his wing and teaches him a trade that pays far better than the neighborhood boys could possibly dream. Striking out on his own, Jacob familiarizes himself with London's highest value neighborhoods while forging his own path in the shadows. But everything changes when he adopts an aspiring teenage thief named Bill Sikes, whose mercurial temper poses a danger to himself and anyone foolish enough to cross him. Along the way, Jacob's found family expands to include his closest friend, Nancy, and his greatest protege, the Artful Dodger. But as Bill's ambition soars and a major robbery goes awry, Jacob is forced to decide what he really stands for--and what a life is worth. --Provided by the publisherFagin the Thief _________ An ASL (American Sign Language) interpreter can be provided for this program if requested at least seven business days in advance. For assistance, please contact your library or accessibility@aclibrary.org.

events.title: Cookbook Book Club
events.description: Do you love books and food? Are you looking for a fun, low-cost way to increase your cooking skills, try new recipes and make some new foodie friends? Then perhaps our new "Cookbook Book Club" is for you!Our book club hostess is Chef JoEllen Hothem. She is a self-educated home chef who began exploring food ways and cooking as a child through the "Mud Pies and Other Recipes" by Marjorie Winslow. She loves to learn about world cuisines through reading and travel. A firm believer in the tremendous health benefits of intentional, healthful eating, in community, she combines her love of food with her public speaking and instructional training to help people learn the principles of taste, flavor and foundational cooking skills, so they can confidently prepare tasty and healthful meals for themselves, family and friends.

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: The Husbands
events.description: In April the Cupertino LIbrary Book Discussion Group will talk about the comic novel The Husbands by Holly Gramazio (2024) For months, Lauren a single woman in her thirties has been coming home to a new, unrecognizable young man who claims to be her husband. In each case, she discovers his story seems to be true-the photos and texts in her phone all include him, yet each time she sends one of these husbands up to her attic, he is almost immediately replaced by a new "husband." And the content of her photos and her text logs change to show the new man, also. Bewildered and exhausted, Lauren wonders how she can possibly figure out which husband is the right man she truly wants to commit to?The Cupertino Adult Book Discussion meets on the third Wednesday of each month at 11am and the third Thursday evening at 7pm. All meetings are held in Room 201B. Adults of all ages are welcome. All meetings are sponsored by The Friends of the Cupertino Library. For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Lost Girls of Paris
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Join our book club for spirited discussions on a wide range of fiction and non-fiction. Each month we'll tackle a new title. Whether you loved it, loathed it, or left it unfinished, we want to hear your take. Come share your impressions and connect with fellow readers! This week we will be reading The Lost Girls of Paris by Pam Jenoff. Three women. One daring mission.Manhattan, 1946. One morning while passing through Grand Central Terminal, Grace Healey finds an abandoned suitcase tucked beneath a bench. Inside is a dozen photographs—each of a different woman. Grace soon learns that the suitcase belonged to Eleanor Trigg, leader of a network of female secret agents deployed out of London during the war. Twelve of these women were sent to Occupied Europe as couriers and radio operators to aid the resistance, but they never returned home.In this riveting story inspired by true events, Pam Jenoff weaves a tale of courage, sisterhood and the great strength of women to survive in the hardest of circumstances.

You will be asked to... Prepare program materialsTidy up program materials Providing 1-1 or small group support during an activity or craftRole model appropriate behaviour Registration Required. Want to know more about volunteering at the Library? Visit our website! As a Library volunteer, you are expected to... Be on time and reliable – Show up when you’re supposed to and follow through with your responsibilities.Be responsible and dedicated – Do your tasks well and take them seriously.Respect the staff and follow their lead – Listen to your supervisors and do what they ask.Dress and act appropriately – Wear the right clothes and behave in a professional way at all times.Keep things private – Don’t share personal information about patrons, staff, or other volunteers.

events.title: Readers' Book Club
events.description: Join our Readers' Book Club to share the joy of reading and to expand your reading horizons. We will read thought-provoking adult fiction of various genres for readers of different ages and background. We will be reading five different titles in 2026:The Snows of Kilimanjaro and other stories by Ernest Hemingway Unlikely Animals by Annie Hartnett, a Silicon Valley Reads fiction selectionStation Eleven by Emily St John Mandell The Good Lord Bird by James McBride Black Cake by Charmaine Wilkerson What to Expect: For the month of April, we will be reading The Good Lord Bird by James McBride. This novel follows Henry Shackleford, a young slave living in the Kansas Territory in 1856 -- a battleground between anti- and pro- slavery forces -- when legendary abolitionist John Brown arrives. When an argument between Brown and Henry's master turns violent, Henry is forced to leave town -- along with Brown, who believes Henry to be a girl and his good luck charm.Read along to find out what happens and we will discuss The Good Lord Bird, together. I am looking forward to hear what you think.ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Main Book Club
events.description: Join the Main Book Club in the Olympic Room to discuss the novel, Piranesi by Susanna Clarke.About the book: Piranesi’s house is no ordinary building: its rooms are infinite, its corridors endless, its walls are lined with thousands upon thousands of statues, each one different from all the others. Within the labyrinth of halls an ocean is imprisoned; waves thunder up staircases; rooms are flooded in an instant. But Piranesi is not afraid; he understands the tides as he understands the pattern of the labyrinth itself. He lives to explore the house.But as Piranesi explores, evidence emerges of another person, and a terrible truth begins to unravel, revealing a world beyond the one Piranesi has always known.Copies of Piranesi are available at Main while they last for sign out, or from various branch collections.

events.title: Intermediate ESL Book Club
events.description: Are you learning English and want to practice reading and speaking in a group setting? Then the Intermediate ESL Book Club is the place for you! Join other adults learning English as a Second Language in this fun and engaging club. You'll read one book a month and then come discuss it with the rest of the club. Be ready to read your favorite passages to the group! If the books on this list look too easy, try our Advanced ESL Book Club. Here's the reading list: January: Ghost Boys February: The House on Mango Street March: Come on in April: Soul Lanterns May: The Breadwinner June: Paper Wishes July: The Lost Library August: The Birchbark House September: Rosa Parks, My Story October: The Elephant Girl November: A Night Divided December: The One and Only Ivan Any person needing a reasonable accommodation for a disability to access the benefits of the Library’s services, programs, or activities under the Americans with Disabilities Act should contact the Library by telephone at (972) 292-5669, by email at library@friscotexas.gov, or in writing, not less than five (5) business days prior to the program.

events.title: Intermediate ESL Book Club - Chains (The Seeds of America Trilogy)
events.description: Our intermediate ESL Book Club will read Chains (The Seeds of America Trilogy) by Laurie Halse Anderson. This book club is best for intermediate English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then we will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Roberta Linsky. Meeting dates: February 26; March 5, 12, 19; April 2, 16; May 7. (No class March 26; April 9, 23, 30.)Meeting time: Thursdays from 10:00am - 11:00amWhere: Tables by the wall posters Registration is open now! Book club is limited to 10 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting. Book description:As the Revolutionary War begins, thirteen-year-old Isabel wages her own fight...for freedom. Promised freedom upon the death of their owner, she and her sister, Ruth, in a cruel twist of fate become the property of a malicious New York City couple, the Locktons, who have no sympathy for the American Revolution and even less for Ruth and Isabel. When Isabel meets Curzon, a slave with ties to the Patriots, he encourages her to spy on her owners, who know details of British plans for invasion. She is reluctant at first, but when the unthinkable happens to Ruth, Isabel realizes her loyalty belongs to whoever can provide her with freedom.

events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - Animal Farm
events.description: ESL Book Club will read Animal Farm by George Orwell. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: April 16, 23; May 7, 14, 21, 28. No class April 30th.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 7 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.Animal Farm:George Orwell's timeless and timely allegorical novel—a scathing satire of a downtrodden society’s blind march towards totalitarianism.“All animals are equal, but some animals are more equal than others.”A farm is taken over by its overworked, mistreated animals. With flaming idealism and stirring slogans, they set out to create a paradise of progress, justice, and equality. Thus the stage is set for one of the most telling satiric fables ever penned—a razor-edged fairy tale for grown-ups that records the evolution from revolution against tyranny to a totalitarianism just as terrible.When Animal Farm was first published, Stalinist Russia was seen as its target. Today it is devastatingly clear that wherever and whenever freedom is attacked, under whatever banner, the cutting clarity and savage comedy of George Orwell’s masterpiece have a meaning and message still ferociously fresh.

events.description: The Civic Center Library's Morning Mystery Book Club will meet on Thursday, April 16th from 11:00am-12:00pm to discuss The Thursday Murder Club by Richard Osman. While this mystery book club generally meets on the 2nd Thursday of every month from 11:00-12:00n, occasionally the day or time will change. All are welcome to join! Request a copy of the book online or ask at the Info Desk of the Civic Center Library (or call 415-473-6057) for assistance in placing a hold. The Thursday Murder Club (book) or The Thursday Murder Club (eBook) or The Thursday Murder Club (eAudiobook) or The Thursday Murder Club (preloaded audiobook, aka Playaway) or The Thursday Murder Club (large print) by Richard Osman In a peaceful retirement village, four unlikely friends meet weekly in the Jigsaw Room to discuss unsolved crimes; together they call themselves the ‘Thursday Murder Club’. When a local developer is found dead with a mysterious photograph left next to the body, the Thursday Murder Club suddenly find themselves in the middle of their first live case. As the bodies begin to pile up, can our unorthodox but brilliant gang catch the killer, before it's too late? - adapted from the publisher ADA Accommodation Request | Solicitud de Adaptación ADA

events.title: Book Club Discussion - A Guardian and a Thief
events.description: Want to connect with others over the love of reading? Join our book club to discuss Megha Majumdar's A Guardian and a Thief. This title should be on shelves and ready to borrow approximately 5 weeks prior to the discussion date. Check with a staff member if you have questions on how to access a copy.Book Description"In a near-future Kolkata beset by flooding and blight, Ma, her two year old daughter Mishti, and her elderly father Dadu are just days from leaving the collapsing city behind to join Ma's husband in the home he has been building for them in Ann Arbor, Michigan. After procuring long-awaited passports and visas from the consulate, they pack their bags for the flight to America. But in the morning they awaken to discover that Ma's purse, with all the treasured documents within it, has been stolen. A Guardian and a Thief tells two stories: the story of Ma and her family, their struggle to emigrate to America, and their devastation in the wake of the theft that changes their fate to one of implacable tragedy; and Boomba, the thief, whose hunger and desperation to care for his family drive him to commit a crime whose consequences he cannot fathom. With stunning control and command, Megha Majumdar paints a kaleidoscopic portrait of two families whose destinies become inexorably entangled, wresting compassion from each narrative as the complexities of each character's circumstances-their helplessness in the face of poverty and corruption, and the need to stave off encroaching catastrophe--are captured with clarity and piercing empathy. A masterful new work from one of the most exciting voices of her generation"--Provided by publisher.

events.title: Virtual Author Talk: Alex Hutchinson
events.description: The Explorer’s Gene: Why We Seek Big Challenges, New Flavors, and the Blank Spots on the Map examines the deep human impulse to seek the unknown. In this book, Alex Hutchinson draws on neuroscience, psychology, and vivid stories of adventure to show how exploration has shaped our species. He argues that this drive is not merely a pastime but a fundamental part of who we are, one that has guided humanity from ancient voyages to modern quests for meaning. Hutchinson also examines how today’s digital world can mimic novelty without offering true growth, contrasting that with the transformative power of real exploration. Through accounts ranging from remote northern rivers to the ocean‑spanning journeys of the Polynesians, he offers a compelling reflection on embracing uncertainty, learning from struggle, and recognizing when the present moment is enough. ---The Santa Clara County Library District is proud to present a free virtual author‑talk series featuring bestselling, award‑winning, and acclaimed writers from around the world, offering live Q&A sessions two to three times a month. Event links are sent about 24 hours in advance (or upon late registration), recordings are available for a limited time, and a full list of upcoming programs can be found here. ---For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.title: Civic Center Afternoon Book Club: 'The Winter Soldier' by Daniel Mason
events.description: The Civic Center Library's Afternoon Book Club will meet in person on Thursday, April 16th from 1:00-2:00 to discuss The Winter Soldier by Daniel Mason. This afternoon book club generally meets on the 3rd Thursday of every month from 1:00-2:00pm. All are welcome to join! Request a copy of the book online or ask at the Info Desk of the Civic Center Library (or call 415-473-6057) for assistance in placing a hold. The Winter Soldier (book) or The Winter Soldier (ebook) or The Winter Soldier (eAudiobook) or The Winter Soldier (Audiobook on CD) or The Winter Soldier (large print) by Daniel Mason Vienna, 1914. Lucius is a twenty-two-year-old medical student when World War I explodes across Europe. Enraptured by romantic tales of battlefield surgery, he enlists, expecting a position at a well-organized field hospital. But when he arrives at a commandeered church tucked away high in a remote valley of the Carpathian Mountains, he finds a freezing outpost ravaged by typhus. The other doctors have fled, and only a single, mysterious nurse named Sister Margarete remains.But Lucius has never lifted a surgeon's scalpel. And as the war rages across the winter landscape, he finds himself falling in love with the woman from whom he must learn a brutal, makeshift medicine. Then one day, an unconscious soldier is brought in from the snow, his uniform stuffed with strange drawings. He seems beyond rescue, until Lucius makes a fateful decision that will change the lives of doctor, patient, and nurse forever.From the gilded ballrooms of Imperial Vienna to the frozen forests of the Eastern Front; from hardscrabble operating rooms to battlefields thundering with Cossack cavalry, this is a story of war and medicine, of family and finding love, and of the mistakes we make and the precious opportunities to atone - adapted from the publisher ADA Accommodation Request | Solicitud de Adaptación ADA

events.title: Book Club Discussion - The Names
events.description: Want to connect with others over the love of reading? Join our book club to discuss Florence Knapp's The Names. This title should be on shelves and ready to borrow approximately 5 weeks prior to the discussion date. Check with a staff member if you have questions on how to access a copy.Book Description"A dazzling debut that asks: Can a name shape the course of a life? In the wake of an enormous, history-making storm, Cora sets off with her nine-year-old daughter, Maia, to register her son's birth. Her husband Gordon, a local doctor, respected in the community but a terrifying and controlling presence at home, intends for her to follow his family tradition going back generations, and name the child Gordon. But on the journey there, Cora wonders if it's right to impose the burden of this name and its legacy onto her tiny newborn son. She herself has Julian in mind, and Maia offers up her own suggestion: Bear. What follows are three alternate and alternating versions of both Cora's life and her young son's life shaped by her brave, last-minute choice of name. Spanning thirty-five years, the novel draws us in from the first page, as we follow three unforgettable journeys of one young man, but also his mother, grandmother, and sister. In richly layered prose, The Names explores the painful ripple effects of domestic abuse, the messy ties of family, and the possibilities of autonomy and healing."--Provided by publisher.

events.title: Read the World: A Middle Grade Book Club
events.description: Read the World is a book discussion group for youth in 6th to 9th grade. Each month we'll read a book set in a different country and enjoy conversations about the book as well as other cultures and experiences. We'll read from multiple categories, including fiction, nonfiction, graphic novels, novels in verse, and more. This month we'll be reading and discussing The Last Mapmaker by Christina Soontornvat and set in a fantastical Thailand. Please register then stop by the library for a free copy of the book no earlier than one month before the discussion.A high-seas adventure set in a Thai-inspired fantasy world. This is the story of a young woman's struggle to unburden herself of the past and chart her own destiny in a world of secrets. As assistant to Mangkon's most celebrated mapmaker, twelve-year-old Sai plays the part of a well-bred young lady with a glittering future. In reality, her father is a conman - and in a kingdom where the status of one's ancestors dictates their social position, the truth could ruin her. Sai seizes the chance to join an expedition to chart the southern seas, but she isn't the only one aboard with secrets. When Sai learns that the ship might be heading for the fabled Sunderlands - a land of dragons, dangers, and riches beyond imagining - she must weigh the cost of her dreams. Vivid, suspenseful, and thought-provoking, this tale of identity and integrity is as intricate as the maps of old. The Last Mapmaker An ASL (American Sign Language) interpreter can be provided for this program if requested at least seven (7) business days in advance. events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Anxious People
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This month we are reading Anxious People by Fredrik Backman Thomas. An apartment open house becomes a life-or-death situation when a failed bank robber bursts in and takes eight extremely anxious strangers hostage. The captives include a retired couple who hunt down fixer-uppers to avoid the painful truth that they can't fix up their own marriage, a wealthy banker who has been too busy making money to care about anyone else, a young couple who are about to have their first baby but can't seem to agree on anything, an eighty-year old woman who has lived long enough not to be afraid of someone waving a gun in her face, a flustered, but still-ready-to-make-a-deal real estate agent, and a mystery man who has locked himself in the apartment's only bathroom. As the authorities and media surround the premises, these reluctant allies will reveal surprising truths about themselves and set in motion a chain of events so surprising that even they can't explain what happens next.

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: The Husbands
events.description: In April the Cupertino LIbrary Book Discussion Group will talk about the comic novel The Husbands by Holly Gramazio (2024) For months, Lauren a single woman in her thirties has been coming home to a new, unrecognizable young man who claims to be her husband. In each case, she discovers his story seems to be true-the photos and texts in her phone all include him, yet each time she sends one of these husbands up to her attic, he is almost immediately replaced by a new "husband." And the content of her photos and her text logs change to show the new man, also. Bewildered and exhausted, Lauren wonders how she can possibly figure out which husband is the right man she truly wants to commit to?The Cupertino Adult Book Discussion meets on the third Wednesday of each month at 11am and the third Thursday evening at 7pm. All meetings are held in Room 201B. Adults of all ages are welcome. All meetings are sponsored by The Friends of the Cupertino Library. For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@sccl.org.

events.title: Intermediate ESL Book Club - Caleb's Story
events.description: Our intermediate ESL Book Club will read Caleb's Story by Patricia MacLachlan. This book club is best for intermediate English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then we will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Elaine Hilbelink. Meeting dates: April 17; May 1, 8, 15, 29. No class April 24th or May 22nd. Meeting time: Fridays 10am - 11am Where: Literacy Table Registration is open now! Book club is limited to 10 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting. Caleb's Story description: The third book in the series that began with the Newbery Medal–winning Sarah, Plain and Tall by Patricia MacLachlan. Anna has done something terrible. She has given me a journal to fill. "It's your job now," Anna says as she hands Caleb her journals, asking him to continue writing the family story. But Sarah, Jacob, Anna, Caleb, and their new little sister, Cassie, have already formed a family, and Caleb fears there will be nothing left to write about. That is, before Cassie discovers a mysterious old man in the barn, and everything changes. Everyone is excited about the arrival of a new family member—except for Jacob, who holds a bitter grudge. Only the special love of Caleb, and the gift he offers, can help to mend the pain of the past.

events.title: Intermediate ESL Book Club - The Game
events.description: Our intermediate ESL Book Club will read The Game by Monica Hughes. This book club is best for intermediate English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then we will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Rianne Hastie Adams. Meeting dates: February 27; March 6, 20, 27; April 3, 17. (No class March 13 and April 10.)Meeting time: Fridays 11:30am - 12:30pm Where: Literacy Table Registration is open now! Book club is limited to 10 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting. The Game description:It’s the year 2154. Lisse and her friends have been deemed unemployable in the eyes of society. Now they must scavenge the disintegrating city for food and shelter, just to make ends meet. But their dismal existence starts to look up when Lisse and her friends are invited to participate in The Game, a virtual reality experience where they are challenged to survive. But over time, the line between reality and fantasy starts to blur. What started as a simple exercise quickly becomes a test of endurance, trust, and their will to live.

events.title: Albany Library Saturday Afternoon Book Club- The Mighty Red
events.description: We welcome you to Albany Library’s monthly book club discussion. The club meets every third Saturday to discuss selecting reading materials. Materials selected for reading and discussion are to be determined by the whole club. The group is open to adults as well as teens, and the number of participants is 12. Please consider joining us for this event. Pick of the Month: The Mighty Red is by Louise Erdrich. The Mighty Red is a 2024 novel by Louise Erdrich set in the Red River Valley of North Dakota during the 2008 economic crisis, focusing on a community entangled in the sugar beet industry, climate change, and personal dramas, particularly a love triangle involving high school graduates Kismet, Gary, and Hugo, and Kismet's mother, Crystal, exploring themes of love, loss, heritage, and the land. The story blends humor and tragedy as it follows characters navigating difficult circumstances, including a fraught wedding and the impact of industrial farming on the environment. Remember, this is a safe space where members provide meaningful feedback to all who participate.Seating: First Come Serve Basis. Library staff may take photos, videos, or audio recordings at our events. Please tell us if you do not wish to be photographed, video-recorded, or audio-recorded. We will always respect your requests! An ASL (American Sign Language) interpreter or closed captioning can be provided for this program if requested at least seven (7) days in advance. For assistance, please contact your library or accessibility@aclibrary.org.

events.title: Adv. 3D Printing for Youth
events.description: Ready to take your 3D design skills to the next level? Join us at Advanced 3D Printing for Youth! In this club, we’ll dive deeper into 3D modeling techniques, troubleshoot designs, and experiment with more advanced tools. Whether you want to create functional gadgets, detailed miniatures, or creative prints, this is your chance to push your understanding of 3D printing! EPL staff will provide guidance and feedback as you refine your designs! Each participant’s final design will be 3D printed for free and available for pickup at your local EPL branch. Recommended for ages 9-15 with prior experience in TinkerCAD or 3D design. If attendees want to create an object larger than 6cm x 6cm x 6cm, parents must complete the certification and bring a USB to the class so that the Makerspace can support with processing the print! To 3D print at the Makerspace: An adult (or youth aged 12+) must be certified to 3D print, which is a quick online process found on our website!Book the printer by calling 780-944-5342, email makerspace@epl.ca, or chat face to face with Makerspace staff!Slice the design in the Makerspace, in Children's library, in our class, or on your own devices!Bring the file on a USB to the Makerspace to process. Other branches with on-the-floor 3D printing services are: Abbottsfield, Mill Woods, and Capilano!

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Excellent Women
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Join our book club for spirited discussions on a wide range of fiction and non-fiction. Each month we'll tackle a new title. Whether you loved it, loathed it, or left it unfinished, we want to hear your take. Come share your impressions and connect with fellow readers! This week we will be reading Excellent Women by Barbara Pym. "A hilarious comedy of manners by the shrewdly observant British novelist often compared to Jane AustenOne of Barbara Pym's richest and most amusing high comedies, Excellent Women has at its center Mildred Lathbury, a clergyman's daughter and a mild-mannered spinster in 1950s England. She is one of those "excellent women," the smart, supportive, repressed women who men take for granted. As Mildred gets embroiled in the lives of her new neighbors--anthropologist Helena Napier and her handsome, dashing husband, Rocky, and Julian Malory, the vicar next door--the novel presents a series of snapshots of human life as actually, and pluckily, lived in a vanishing world of manners and repressed desires.”

events.title: Unplugged Book Club
events.description: (This is a special Unplugged Book Club session where we will be joined by the hosts of the Environmental Book Club at DeGraaf Nature Center) The Unplugged Book Club is a collaborative book discussion series between ODC Network and Herrick District Library. Throughout 2026, we invite you to read books related to disconnecting from unhealthy tech habits and reconnecting with our community and nature. Want to meet some of the authors? Join the ODC Network's Unplugged: Disconnect to Reconnect speaker series to meet Catherine Price (January 14), Katherine Martinko (February 12), Dr. Scott Sampson (March 3), and Lenore Skenazy (March 17).About the Unplugged: Disconnect to Reconnect speaker series - ODC Network invites the West Michigan community to disconnect from overuse of technology and reconnect with nature and each other, while reintroducing the simple idea that outdoor and community connection is central to thriving people, thriving families, and thriving communities. Learn more at outdoordiscovery.org/unplugged Here is our Unplugged Book Club lineup: April 20th, 6:30-8:00p at DeGraaf Nature Center (600 Graafschap Rd) – Braiding Sweetgrass by Robin Wall Kimmerer (Hosted jointly with the Environmental Book Club) ___ May 11th, 6:30 – 7:30p at Herrick Main (classroom) – An Immense World by Ed Yong ___ June 17th, 6:30-7:30p at DeGraaf Nature Center (600 Graafschap Rd) – How to Know a Person by David Brooks ___ July 13th, 6:30-7:30p at Herrick Main (classroom) – A Walk in the Woods by Bill Bryson ___ August 13th, 10:00-11:00a Herrick Main (classroom) – Digital Minimalism by Cal Newport ___ September 17th, 6:30- 7:30p at Herrick North Branch – The Nature Fix by Florence Williams ___ October 7th, 6:00-7:00p at Herrick Main (classroom) – My Side of the Mountain by Jean Craighead George (All ages, with Youth Services) ___ November 10th, 6:00-7:00p at Herrick Main (classroom) – Wild by Cheryl Strayed

events.title: Tea & Robots Book Club
events.description: Have an appetite for science fiction but don't have time to delve into another space opera? Join us every third Monday of the month to discuss the novellas and short story collections of award-winning sci-fi authors. Through this Spring's offerings of stories, readers will explore a range of topics, from androids to first contact, and from post-apocalyptic to Afrofuturism. Registered participants are welcome to pick up their copies of this month’s book at Divoll. For April, we’re reading excerpts from Lake of Souls, St. Louisan author Ann Leckie’s collection of short stories, both previously published and newly released. The eponymous story, “Lake of Souls,” was a Hugo nominee for best novelette. Leckie’s take on the classic first contact plot, it’s about the friendship between a lone human survivor and an extraterrestrial on a search for meaning.For accessibility accommodations, please call 314-241-2288.

events.title: Books on Tap
events.description: We're bringing book discussions to local bars & breweries. Join us to rant and rave about our favorite books or least favorite tropes. Check our staff booklists for recommendations, come with your own, or listen to what others' are reading! No required book or booklist. Ages 21+.

events.title: 星期一下午:西游记 (读书会)
events.description: A book club dedicated to "Journey to the West," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.小朋友们，你们听说过《西游记》吗？ 这是中国最有名的神话故事之一，讲述了一段惊险又有趣的旅程。在这个故事里，有聪明勇敢的孙悟空、善良慈悲的唐僧、憨厚搞笑的猪八戒，还有忠诚可靠的沙和尚。他们一路上经历了许多危险和考验，但靠着智慧和团结，成功完成了任务。这次旅程不仅是他们追寻经书的故事，更是一场关于勇气、坚持和成长的冒险！更多有关 美猴王系列丛书. 免费的亲子中文活動. 座位数量有限. 门票将在故事时间开始前 30 分钟在询问处领取。 圖書館中文资源: 大人最新中文书, 中文電子書, 非小說類電子書 (Overdrive) 有歡迎大家一起來借閱電子書. Overdirve平台: 小朋友的中文電子書, 大朋友的中文電子小說, 大朋友的非小說類的電子書籍. Hoopla平台: 中文有聲書籍 美国残疾人法案(ADA)申请提供协助 5/25/2026

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Magpie Murders
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This week we will be reading Magpie Murders by Anthony Horowitz.When Susan receives crime writer Alan Conway's latest manuscript the editor begins to suspect there is another story hidden in the pages that may involve a real murder.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Last Policeman
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Join us to share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. The Last Policeman by Ben H. Winters When the Earth is doomed by an imminent and unavoidable asteroid collision, New Hampshire homicide detective Hank Palace considers the worth of his job in a world destined to end in six months and investigates a suspicious suicide that nobody else cares about.

events.title: Manchester Book Group
events.description: Pick up the next book club selection at the Manchester library service desk following the previous month's meeting on the 3rd Monday of the month. **NOTE Meetings in January and February each year are held on the FOURTH Monday of the month due to the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day and President's Day holidays This month we are discussing: Dear Edward by Ann Napolitano.From the Publisher:What does it mean not just to survive, but to truly live?One summer morning, twelve-year-old Edward Adler, his beloved older brother, his parents, and 183 other passengers board a flight in Newark headed for Los Angeles. Among them are a Wall Street wunderkind, a young woman coming to terms with an unexpected pregnancy, an injured veteran returning from Afghanistan, a business tycoon, and a free-spirited woman running away from her controlling husband. Halfway across the country, the plane crashes. Edward is the sole survivor.Edward’s story captures the attention of the nation, but he struggles to find a place in a world without his family. He continues to feel that a part of himself has been left in the sky, forever tied to the plane and all of his fellow passengers. But then he makes an unexpected discovery—one that will lead him to the answers of some of life’s most profound questions: When you’ve lost everything, how do you find the strength to put one foot in front of the other? How do you learn to feel safe again? How do you find meaning in your life?

events.title: Inclusive Storytime and Author Talk with Veronica Vásquez García ~ CUENTOS Y PLATICA CON AUTOR SENSORIAL
events.description: As part of Gilroy Library and JMM Dance Co.'s celebration of National Autism Acceptance Month, author and instructor Veronica Vásquez García will be leading an inclusive storytime for families on April 20th at 6pm. This storytime will be in place of our weekly Pajama Storytime and will feature My Emotions Dance Journal, introducing simple movement-based emotional regulation strategies families can use at home. Veronica is a bilingual somatic educator, teaching artist, and nonprofit leader with over 20 years of experience using movement to build confidence, connection, and emotional regulation. Learn more about her and her work at her website here: Home - Veronica Vasquez Garcia Gilroy Library will also be hosting 2 Sensory Movement & Regulation Workshops with JMM Dance Co. on April 12th and 19th that families can register for and learn more about here: Inclusive Sensory Movement & Regulation Workshop | Events | Santa Clara County LibraryInclusive Sensory Movement & Regulation Workshop | Events | Santa Clara County Library Across these three events, the first 30 children who register can receive a free copy of a JMM Dance Co. children’s book to support continued learning at home. These include: ● My Emotions Dance Journal (recommended for sensory regulation and emotional literacy)● Elinor’s Princess Pre-Ballet Book and/or Eli’s Hip Hop Tumble Book As the storytime on the 20th does not require registration, any books left over from the workshops will be given to the first families to show up and sign in for the storytime. Learn more about JMM Dance Co. and their work at their website here: JMM Dance Co.For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.EN ESPAÑOLComo parte de la celebración del Mes Nacional de la Aceptación del Autismo de la Biblioteca de Gilroy y la Compañía de Danza JMM, la autora e instructora Verónica Vásquez García ofrecerá una hora de cuentos inclusiva para familias el 20 de abril a las 6 p. m. Esta hora de cuentos reemplazará nuestra Hora de Cuentos en Pijama semanal y presentará el Diario de Danza "Mis Emociones", que presenta estrategias sencillas de regulación emocional basadas en el movimiento que las familias pueden usar en casa. Verónica es una educadora somática bilingüe, artista docente y líder de organizaciones sin fines de lucro con más de 20 años de experiencia utilizando el movimiento para desarrollar la confianza, la conexión y la regulación emocional. Conozca más sobre ella y su trabajo en su sitio web aquí: Inicio - Verónica Vásquez García La Biblioteca de Gilroy también ofrecerá dos talleres de Movimiento y Regulación Sensorial con la Compañía de Danza JMM los días 12 y 19 de abril. Las familias pueden inscribirse y obtener más información aquí: Taller de Movimiento y Regulación Sensorial Inclusiva | Eventos | Biblioteca del Condado de Santa Clara Taller de Movimiento y Regulación Sensorial Inclusiva | Eventos | Biblioteca del Condado de Santa Clara En estos tres eventos, los primeros 30 niños que se inscriban recibirán una copia gratuita de un libro infantil de JMM Dance Co. para apoyar el aprendizaje continuo en casa. Estos incluyen: ● Diario de Danza "Mis Emociones" (recomendado para la regulación sensorial y la alfabetización emocional) ● Libro de Pre-Ballet "La Princesa" de Elinor y/o Libro de Hip Hop "Tumble" de Eli Como la hora del cuento del día 20 no requiere inscripción, los libros sobrantes de los talleres se entregarán a las primeras familias que se inscriban. Para obtener más información sobre JMM Dance Co. y su trabajo, visite su sitio web: JMM Dance Co. Para adaptaciones de accesibilidad (por ejemplo, Lenguaje de Señas Americano), complete el formulario en línea en sccld.org/accessibility con al menos 7 días de anticipación al evento. Si necesita ayuda con el formulario, hable con un miembro del personal de su biblioteca local, llame al (408) 540-3945 o envíenos un correo electrónico a ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.title: Reading Bingo
events.description: Join us in celebrating Día del Niño (Day of the Child) at the Union City Library throughout April. Pick up a "Reading Bingo" card at the reference desk and complete five reading activities across or diagonally on your own or with a reading friend. Show us your completed bingo card at the reference desk by Saturday, May 2, 2026, for a small prize, while supplies last. Día del Niño (Day of the Child) is celebrated in Mexico every year on April 30. It is a holiday that highlights the wonder and joy children bring into our lives. The library has adapted Día del Niño; we celebrate Día del Niño & Libro by promoting reading and children’s literacy. Acompáñanos a celebrar el Día del Niño en la Biblioteca de Union City durante todo el mes de abril. Recoge tu tarjeta de "Reading Bingo" en el mostrador de consulta y completa cinco actividades de lectura tú solo o con un compañero. Enséñanos tu tarjeta terminada y recibirás un premio pequeño mientras duran existencias. El Día del Niño es celebrado en México cada 30 de abril. Es un día festivo que resalta la maravilla y alegría que los niños comparten con nosotros. El Día del Niño ha sido adaptado por la biblioteca, celebraremos el Día del Niño & Libro promoviendo la lectura y alfabetización infantil. Library staff may take photos, videos, or audio recordings at our events. Please tell us if you do not wish to be photographed, video-recorded, or audio-recorded. We will always respect your requests! An ASL (American Sign Language) interpreter or closed captioning can be provided for this program if requested at least seven (7) days in advance. For assistance, please contact your library or accessibility@aclibrary.org.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Shuggie Bain
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This week we will be reading Shuggie Bain by Douglas StuartShuggie Bain is the unforgettable story of young Hugh "Shuggie" Bain, a sweet and lonely boy who spends his 1980s childhood in run-down public housing in Glasgow, Scotland. Thatcher's policies have put husbands and sons out of work, and the city's notorious drugs epidemic is waiting in the wings.A heartbreaking story of addiction, sexuality, and love,Shuggie Bain is an epic portrayal of a working-class family that is rarely seen in fiction. Recalling the work of Édouard Louis, Alan Hollinghurst, Frank McCourt, and Hanya Yanagihara, it is a blistering debut by a brilliant novelist who has a powerful and important story to tell.

events.title: Swasey Book Club
events.description: Join us for the Swasey Book Club! April's selection is The Frozen River by Ariel Lawhon. About the book:"A gripping historical mystery based on the real-life diary entries of Martha Ballard, an 18th-century midwife who defied the legal system and wrote herself into American history. Maine, 1789: When the Kennebec River freezes, entombing a man in the ice, Martha Ballard is summoned to examine the body and determine cause of death. As a midwife and healer, she is privy to much of what goes on behind closed doors in Hallowell. Her diary is a record of every birth and death, crime and debacle that unfolds in the close-knit community. When a local physician undermines her conclusion, declaring the death to be an accident, Martha is forced to investigate the shocking murder on her own. Over the course of one winter, as the trial nears, and whispers and prejudices mount, Martha doggedly pursues the truth. Her diary soon lands at the center of the scandal, implicating those she loves, and compelling Martha to decide where her own loyalties lie." Stop into the Swasey branch to secure your book club copy and then join us for the discussion on April 21. We hope you'll join us! The Swasey Book Club selection checks out on a first-come, first-served basis. Supply is limited. Ask library staff for assistance. Call us at (253)280-2970 if you have any questions.
